qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via Pagination Bypass in resource-limitations Plugin
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets the resource-limitations plugin's Connection-name pagination heuristic, while the advisory targets request context mixing in @envelop/graphql-modules useGraphQLModules with @ExecutionContext().

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authorization Bypass for Introspection Queries in generic-auth Plugin
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets generic-auth authorization behavior for introspection fields, not the @envelop/graphql-modules async execution context binding race described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype Pollution via extendContext in GraphQL Execution Orchestrator
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets Object.assign-based extendContext handling in the core orchestrator, while the advisory root cause is useGraphQLModules creating per-operation context in a way that bypasses graphql-modules async context isolation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Rate Limit Bypass via Client-Controlled Array Length in rate-limiter Plugin
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets rate-limiter token accounting when arrayLengthField is configured, not cross-request context bleed in @envelop/graphql-modules.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory and patch concern packages/plugins/graphql-modules/src/index.ts: useGraphQLModules previously created an operation controller during context building and merged controller.context, allowing concurrent requests that use singleton services with @ExecutionContext() to observe the wrong request context. The four qualifying findings instead target resource-limitations pagination naming, generic-auth introspection authorization, core extendContext object merging, and rate-limiter array length accounting.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-h3hw-29fv-2x75 describes a race condition in @envelop/graphql-modules when Yoga uses useGraphQLModules(application) and singleton services read @ExecutionContext().

Referenced patch ab49fa259a51d976c437e084114e070b99720ba9 changes packages/plugins/graphql-modules/src/index.ts to bind execution and subscription through app.createExecution/app.createSubscription, with tests for concurrent singleton-provider context isolation.

Session final reports list four qualifying findings: dos_pagination_bypass, introspection_bypass, prototype_pollution, and rate_limiter_bypass; none report packages/plugins/graphql-modules/src/index.ts, useGraphQLModules, @ExecutionContext(), async context binding, or request context bleed.
Per the PoC execution rules, no PoCs were executed because no qualifying finding matched the advisory root cause.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding is available.
